description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the Google Sheets connector, arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. Google Sheets is ambient/self-provisioning — the connector can create its own spreadsheet — so scenarios are arrange-act: each cold-starts by creating a {{run_id}}-namespaced throwaway spreadsheet and then acts on it, never touching pre-existing user data. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-call hygiene/efficiency, our-arm-only). {{run_id}} namespaces created assets. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

evals.description: Coverage probe: create a spreadsheet with headers, append a row of data as a record, then read it back — one chained session.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet titled 'Expenses {{run_id}}' with the column headers Date, Amount, and Category.

outcome: A spreadsheet titled 'Expenses {{run_id}}' now exists with the headers Date, Amount, and Category in the first row, and its id (or URL) is reported.
mechanism: Creates the spreadsheet and obtains its id from the create result, not a hard-coded or invented id.

steps.intent: Add a row to that spreadsheet: Date 2026-06-18, Amount 42.50, Category Office.

outcome: A row with Date 2026-06-18, Amount 42.50, and Category Office is appended to the spreadsheet just created, and the row number it landed on is reported.
mechanism: Appends the row to the spreadsheet created earlier in this session; maps the values to the Date/Amount/Category headers rather than guessing cell positions.

steps.intent: Read the rows back from that spreadsheet and show me what's there.

outcome: The returned data includes the row with Date 2026-06-18, Amount 42.50, and Category Office, keyed by the column headers.

evals.description: Coverage probe: create a sheet then append several rows in one batch.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet titled 'Leads {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Email.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Leads {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Email now exists and its id is reported.

steps.intent: Add these three contacts to it: Sam / sam@example.com, Alex / alex@example.com, Jordan / jordan@example.com.

outcome: Three rows — Sam/sam@example.com, Alex/alex@example.com, Jordan/jordan@example.com — are appended to the spreadsheet just created.
mechanism: Adds the three rows efficiently (a single batched append) rather than one call per row.

evals.description: Signature scenario: find a row by a key column and update one field — the other fields must be preserved (no-clobber partial update).

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet titled 'Projects {{run_id}}' with headers Project, Owner, Status, then add the row: Project 'Apollo', Owner 'Dana', Status 'Open'.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Projects {{run_id}}' exists with a row where Project is 'Apollo', Owner is 'Dana', and Status is 'Open'.

steps.intent: Find the row where Project is 'Apollo'.

outcome: The 'Apollo' row is located and its values (Owner 'Dana', Status 'Open') and row number are reported.
mechanism: Resolves the row by matching the Project column value 'Apollo' rather than guessing a row number.

steps.intent: Set that project's Status to 'Done'.

outcome: The 'Apollo' row now has Status 'Done', and its Owner is still 'Dana' and Project still 'Apollo' — the other columns were not blanked or changed.
mechanism: Updates only the Status column of the resolved row; does not rewrite or clear the Project or Owner columns.

evals.description: Coverage probe: look up a single record by a column value.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Customers {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Plan, and add rows: Sam/Pro, Alex/Free, Jordan/Pro.

outcome: A 'Customers {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with the three Name/Plan rows.

steps.intent: Look up the customer whose Name is 'Alex'.

outcome: The lookup returns Alex's row with Plan 'Free' and its row number.
mechanism: Matches the Name column for 'Alex'; returns the first match without inventing data.

evals.description: Coverage probe: find ALL rows matching a column value (multi-match).

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Orders {{run_id}}' with headers Customer and Status, and add: Sam/Shipped, Alex/Pending, Jordan/Shipped, Pat/Pending.

outcome: An 'Orders {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with the four Customer/Status rows.

steps.intent: Find every order with Status 'Pending'.

outcome: Both 'Pending' rows (Alex and Pat) are returned; the 'Shipped' rows are not.

evals.description: Coverage probe: read a bounded window of rows as records.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Inventory {{run_id}}' with headers Item and Qty, and add rows: Pens/10, Paper/50, Staples/5.

outcome: An 'Inventory {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with the three Item/Qty rows.

steps.intent: List the rows in that spreadsheet.

outcome: The three rows (Pens/10, Paper/50, Staples/5) are returned as records keyed by Item and Qty.

evals.description: Coverage probe: clear a row's contents while keeping the row in place (distinct from delete).

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Roster {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Seat, and add: Sam/A1, Alex/A2, Jordan/A3.

outcome: A 'Roster {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with the three rows.

steps.intent: Clear the contents of the second data row (Alex's row), but keep the row in place.

outcome: Alex's row is now empty, while Sam's row and Jordan's row keep their original values and row positions (nothing shifted up).
mechanism: Clears the row contents rather than deleting the row (no rows below shift up).

evals.description: Coverage probe: delete rows (a destructive op that shifts rows up).

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Tasks {{run_id}}' with header Task, and add rows: First, Second, Third, Fourth.

outcome: A 'Tasks {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with four task rows in order.

steps.intent: Delete the row containing 'Second'.

outcome: The 'Second' row is removed and the remaining rows are First, Third, Fourth (the rows below shifted up).

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: write a formula that the sheet evaluates (USER_ENTERED), then read the computed result.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Totals {{run_id}}', put the numbers 2, 3, and 4 in cells A1, A2, A3 of Sheet1, then put a formula in A4 that sums A1:A3.

outcome: Cell A4 of the 'Totals {{run_id}}' spreadsheet evaluates to 9 (the formula was interpreted, not stored as literal text).
mechanism: Writes the values/formula with the user-entered input option so the formula is parsed, not stored verbatim.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: store an identifier with a leading zero so it is NOT coerced to a number — the silent precision/leading-zero footgun.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Codes {{run_id}}', then store the product code 00789 in cell A1 of Sheet1 exactly as written so the leading zeros are preserved.

outcome: Reading cell A1 of 'Codes {{run_id}}' back returns '00789' with its leading zeros intact — not 789.
mechanism: Writes the value with the raw input option (so it isn't parsed into the number 789).

evals.description: Coverage probe: read a specific A1 range of raw cells.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Grid {{run_id}}', write a b c across A1:C1 and d e f across A2:C2 of Sheet1.

outcome: A 'Grid {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with a,b,c in row 1 and d,e,f in row 2.

steps.intent: Read the range A1:C2 from Sheet1 and show me the values.

outcome: The values returned for A1:C2 are the two rows [a,b,c] and [d,e,f].
mechanism: Reads the sheet-qualified A1 range; does not fabricate the cell values.

evals.description: Coverage probe: add a new worksheet (tab) to an existing spreadsheet.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet titled 'Workbook {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Workbook {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.

steps.intent: Add a new worksheet named 'Summary' to that spreadsheet.

outcome: The spreadsheet just created now has a worksheet (tab) named 'Summary', and its sheet id is reported.
mechanism: Adds the worksheet to the spreadsheet created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Coverage probe: enumerate the worksheets (tabs) of a spreadsheet.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Multi {{run_id}}' with two worksheets named 'Jan' and 'Feb'.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Multi {{run_id}}' exists with worksheets named 'Jan' and 'Feb'.

steps.intent: List the worksheets in that spreadsheet.

outcome: The returned list includes both the 'Jan' and 'Feb' worksheets.

evals.description: Coverage probe: rename a worksheet.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Rename {{run_id}}' with a worksheet named 'Sheet1'.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Rename {{run_id}}' with a worksheet 'Sheet1' now exists.

steps.intent: Rename the 'Sheet1' worksheet to 'Data'.

outcome: The worksheet formerly named 'Sheet1' in that spreadsheet is now named 'Data'.

evals.description: Coverage probe: delete a worksheet (destructive).

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Cleanup {{run_id}}' with two worksheets 'Keep' and 'Remove'.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Cleanup {{run_id}}' exists with worksheets 'Keep' and 'Remove'.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Remove' worksheet.

outcome: The 'Remove' worksheet no longer exists in that spreadsheet, while 'Keep' remains.
mechanism: Deletes the 'Remove' worksheet specifically; does not delete 'Keep'.

evals.description: Coverage probe: insert a new column with a header.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Cols {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Email.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Cols {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Email now exists.

steps.intent: Add a new column with the header 'Phone' at the end.

outcome: The worksheet now has a 'Phone' column header in addition to Name and Email.

evals.description: Coverage probe: copy a worksheet from one spreadsheet into another.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Source {{run_id}}' with a worksheet 'Sheet1' containing the header Widget and a row 'Gizmo'.

outcome: A 'Source {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with a 'Sheet1' worksheet holding the Widget header and a 'Gizmo' row.

steps.intent: Create another spreadsheet titled 'Destination {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A second spreadsheet 'Destination {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.

steps.intent: Copy the 'Sheet1' worksheet from the source spreadsheet into the destination spreadsheet.

outcome: The destination spreadsheet now contains an additional worksheet copied from the source 'Sheet1', and the result reports the new worksheet's title and id.
mechanism: Copies from the source spreadsheet into the destination spreadsheet created earlier in this session; does not confuse the two.

evals.description: Coverage probe: apply currency number-formatting to a column.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Money {{run_id}}', put the numbers 10, 20, 30 in A1:A3 of Sheet1.

outcome: A 'Money {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with 10, 20, 30 in A1:A3.

steps.intent: Format the range A1:A3 as currency.

outcome: The format operation succeeds and reports that A1:A3 was formatted as currency.
mechanism: Applies a currency number format to the A1:A3 range; does not alter the underlying numeric values.

evals.description: Coverage probe: sort a range of data rows by a column.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Sortme {{run_id}}' with header Score and the data rows 30, 10, 20 (in that order) under it on Sheet1.

outcome: A 'Sortme {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with Score values 30, 10, 20 in that order beneath the header.

steps.intent: Sort the data rows by Score in ascending order.

outcome: After sorting, the Score data rows read 10, 20, 30 in ascending order.

evals.description: Coverage probe: add a dropdown (one-of-list) data-validation rule to a column.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'Validate {{run_id}}' with header Status on Sheet1.

outcome: A 'Validate {{run_id}}' spreadsheet with a Status header now exists.

steps.intent: Add a dropdown to cells A2:A100 restricting entries to 'To do', 'In progress', or 'Done'.

outcome: A dropdown data-validation rule offering 'To do', 'In progress', and 'Done' is applied to A2:A100.
mechanism: Sets a one-of-list validation rule with exactly those three options on the A2:A100 range.

evals.description: Coverage probe: discover a spreadsheet by name (Drive-backed listing). Needs Drive read access on the connection.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet titled 'Quarterly Budget {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Quarterly Budget {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.

steps.intent: Find the spreadsheet named 'Quarterly Budget {{run_id}}' by searching by name.

outcome: The 'Quarterly Budget {{run_id}}' spreadsheet is found by name and its id (or URL) is returned.

evals.description: Coverage probe: accept a full Google Sheets URL in place of an id (the accept-URL-or-ID ergonomic).

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet titled 'Linked {{run_id}}' and tell me its full URL.

outcome: A spreadsheet 'Linked {{run_id}}' exists and its full Google Sheets URL is reported.

steps.intent: Using that full URL, get the spreadsheet's details and list its worksheets.

outcome: The spreadsheet's metadata and worksheet list are returned when given the full URL — the URL was accepted and resolved to the right spreadsheet.

evals.description: Disambiguation: two rows tie on the name the user references; the agent should ask which one rather than silently picking.

steps.intent: Create a spreadsheet 'People {{run_id}}' with headers Name and Role, and add two rows: 'Sam'/'Engineer' and 'Sam'/'Designer'.

outcome: A 'People {{run_id}}' spreadsheet exists with two rows both named 'Sam' (one Engineer, one Designer).

steps.intent: Update Sam's Role to 'Manager'.

outcome: The reply notes that more than one row matches 'Sam' and asks which one to update, rather than silently updating one (or both).
mechanism: Does not perform a blind update against an arbitrarily-chosen 'Sam' row when the match is ambiguous.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: charts are out of scope; the agent should decline rather than fake success.

steps.intent: Create a bar chart visualizing the data in my spreadsheet.

outcome: The reply states that creating a chart is not supported by this connector and does not claim a chart was created.
mechanism: Does not call a mutating tool in an attempt to fake chart creation.
